meta.project: Chakravyuh - AI War-Room for India Energy Resilience
meta.source_attribution: Ministry of Petroleum, ISPRL, PPAC, S&P Global, EIA, GDELT, AISstream, IMF PortWatch

refineries.name: Jamnagar Refinery Complex (Reliance)
refineries.hormuz_dependency_pct_source: estimated - modeled share of this refinery's crude sourced via the Persian Gulf/Strait of Hormuz, informed by its west-coast port geography and known Gulf-heavy crude slate; not an officially disclosed sourcing-mix figure
refineries.source_attribution: PPAC Ready Reckoner FY2022-23, Table 4.1 (Installed capacity, MMTPA, as of 01.04.2023)

refineries.source_attribution: Verified via HPCL LC-Max Residue Upgradation Facility commissioning, 5 Jan 2026 (raised capacity from 11.0 to 15.0 MMTPA); PPAC Ready Reckoner FY2022-23 Table 4.1 still shows the pre-upgrade 11.0 MMTPA snapshot as of 01.04.2023

chokepoints.name: Strait of Hormuz
chokepoints.status_detail: Fresh US strikes on Iran (11-14 Jul); Iran claims renewed closure and vessel strikes, but US CENTCOM disputes that transit is fully halted - status is genuinely contested between the two sides' public claims. Commercial transit ~11% of pre-crisis baseline per the most recent published tracker day.
chokepoints.source: IMF PortWatch, via CNBC (2026-07-13): ~10 vessels/day vs ~88/day baseline, for PortWatch's most recently published day (12 Jul 2026) as of this 2026-07-20 verification pass - a multi-day reporting lag is normal for this tracker, so this is the freshest confirmed transit count, not a stale figure

chokepoints.name: Bab-el-Mandeb
chokepoints.status_detail: Red Sea rerouting active via Cape of Good Hope
chokepoints.source: estimated - real-world 2026 reporting on Bab-el-Mandeb/Red Sea traffic is inconsistent (some trackers show a rebound toward normal levels post the Oct-2025 Israel-Gaza ceasefire, others still show daily transits well below the pre-2024 baseline), so this project models a mid-range ~50%-of-normal disruption rather than asserting a single verified live figure

chokepoints.name: Cape of Good Hope
chokepoints.status_detail: Alternate maritime transit route; adds 10-14 days transit delay to India
chokepoints.source: modeled - extra_transit_days reflects the well-documented ~10-14 day sailing-distance penalty of the Cape of Good Hope route vs. Hormuz/Suez for India-bound cargo; this chokepoint has no capacity constraint of its own (it is a reroute destination, not a blockaded strait)

timeline_anchors.title: Conflict Outbreak & IRGC Maritime Warnings
timeline_anchors.description: US/Israeli strikes on Iran launch. IRGC begins boarding merchant vessels off Hormuz.

timeline_anchors.title: Formal Closure Declaration
timeline_anchors.description: IRGC declares Strait of Hormuz closed to commercial traffic. Major global shipping lines suspend passage.

timeline_anchors.title: Temporary De-escalation Accord
timeline_anchors.description: US-Iran memorandum signed; temporary surge in transit volumes.

timeline_anchors.title: Fresh Escalation in Strait
timeline_anchors.description: IRGC strike hits container vessel; Iran redeclares transit restriction. US strikes continue.

timeline_anchors.title: Live Status: Contested Waterway
timeline_anchors.description: Strait remains contested: IMF PortWatch's most recently published day (12 Jul) shows ~10 transits/day vs an ~88/day baseline (~11% of normal). Iran claims further vessel strikes and reasserts closure; US CENTCOM disputes that transit is fully halted. India maintains ~74-80 days total reserve buffer.

prices_baseline.brent_usd_bbl_source: EIA Open Data v2 (series RBRTE), fetched live 2026-07-21; EIA daily series lags ~5-6 business days (period 2026-07-13)
prices_baseline.brent_volatility_note: Largest monthly oil-price jump on record (Mar 2026); whipsawed from $126 peak to sub-$70 trough within four months as de-escalation/re-escalation cycles repeated. Volatility, not a fixed level, is the live risk.
prices_baseline.wti_usd_bbl_source: EIA Open Data v2 (series RWTC), fetched live 2026-07-21 (period 2026-07-13)
prices_baseline.india_retail_source: Dated Tier B fallback snapshot only (used if the live scrape fails) — PPAC-published daily retail selling prices as reported by goodreturns.in, verified 2026-07-21. No free/key-free official JSON API exists for Indian pump rates (IOCL/BPCL/HPCL sites are HTML-only; RapidAPI's daily-fuel-prices-in-india listing needs a paid key). ingest/index.js's pollIndiaRetailPrices() scrapes goodreturns.in's server-rendered HTML tables live every 5 min and upgrades the runtime tier to Tier A when it succeeds; this seed value is only the fallback baseline.
